pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent POM version to 3.3.13 to align with the Java 17 migration and Spring Boot 3 requirements.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x requires Java 17 as a baseline; using Spring Boot 2.x with Java 17 is possible but prevents access to Spring Boot 3 features and may cause dependency conflicts with other upgraded libraries.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 17 to target the new JDK baseline.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project must declare Java 17 as the target version to compile and run on JDK 17+ and to satisfy Spring Boot 3's minimum Java version requirement.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven compiler source property to 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: The compiler must be configured to accept Java 17 language features; otherwise, code using Java 17 syntax will fail to compile.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven compiler target property to 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: The compiler must generate bytecode compatible with Java 17; otherwise, the application may fail to run on JDK 17+ or fail to utilize Java 17 features.
